embed-svc/src/main.rs: This file implements a standalone HTTP embedding microservice that loads the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 ONNX model and exposes a POST /embed endpoint for batch text-to-vector conversion. The embed handler tokenizes input texts, runs ONNX inference via the ort Session, performs mean pooling with attention masking for 3D outputs, and applies l2_normalize to produce 384-dimensional unit vectors. A GET /health endpoint reports model name and dimensionality. The server uses Axum with shared AppState (holding a Mutex<Session> and Tokenizer), binds to a configurable EMBED_SVC_PORT, and supports graceful shutdown via shutdown_signal.
hook-shim/src/main.rs: This file is a lightweight Rust shim that acts as the stdin-receiving entry point for Claude Code hook invocations, forwarding payloads to the hook-client daemon over a Unix domain socket. It reads up to 1 MiB from stdin, connects to the UDS at HOOK_CLIENT_SOCK, and uses a simple framed wire protocol (write_msg/read_msg with a 1-byte type + 4-byte big-endian length header). For PreToolUse Read events, it sends a MSG_CACHE_QUERY and waits for a MSG_CACHE_RESPONSE containing a hookSpecificOutput annotation; for all other events, it sends a fire-and-forget MSG_EVENT. The shim exits silently if the daemon is unavailable, ensuring it never blocks Claude.
hooks-client/main.go: This file is the main entry point for the hooks-client Go binary, which intercepts Claude Code hook events from stdin, enriches them with monitor metadata, and forwards them to a monitor HTTP server. It supports an install-hooks subcommand (runInstallHooks) that idempotently registers all hook types into ~/.claude/settings.json, and a daemon subcommand that delegates to runDaemon. The core flow reads JSON from stdin, validates the hook_event_name, checks per-hook toggle config via isHookEnabled (which parses hook_monitor.conf with fail-open semantics), and calls sendToMonitor to POST the payload.
hooks-client/daemon.go: This file implements the hook-client daemon mode, which listens on a Unix domain socket for framed messages from hook-shim instances and forwards enriched events downstream to hooks-store (or the legacy monitor). The runDaemon function sets up signal-based graceful shutdown, accepts connections, and dispatches each to handleDaemonConn which loops reading UDS messages. It manages a connPool of reusable downstream UDS connections with get/put/send methods and retry-on-failure semantics. The processDaemonEvent function validates, enriches (adding _monitor metadata and _cache file stats), wraps events in a HookEvent envelope, and sends via the pool.
hooks-mcp/cmd/hooks-mcp/main.go: This file is the entry point for the hooks-mcp MCP server, which exposes Claude Code hook analytics as Model Context Protocol tools over a stdio transport. It configures connections to Milvus (vector database) and optionally the embed-svc embedding service, performing a health check via checkMilvusHealth before proceeding. A MilvusClient implementing the meili.Searcher interface is created via milvus.NewMilvusClient, and all MCP tools are registered through tools.RegisterAll.
hooks-mcp/internal/dateparse/dateparse.g: This package converts human-friendly date range strings into structured DateRange values containing both Unix timestamps and ISO 8601 strings, suitable for filtering queries in both MeiliSearch and Milvus backends. The ParseRange function handles inputs like "today", "yesterday", "last N days", "last N hours", single dates (YYYY-MM-DD), and date ranges (YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD), all interpreted in UTC.
hooks-mcp/internal/format/format.go: This package provides pure, side-effect-free formatting functions for rendering MCP tool output as human-readable plain text. It includes Table for aligned columnar output, Tree/TreeNode for hierarchical ASCII tree rendering, and BarChart/BarItem for horizontal bar charts. Utility functions FormatDuration, FormatCost, FormatTokens, FormatTimestamp, FormatTimestampISO, and ShortID handle domain-specific formatting of durations, USD costs, token counts, timestamps, and UUID abbreviations.
hooks-mcp/internal/meili/meiliclient.go: This file contains the original MeiliSearch-backed implementation of the Searcher interface, preserved behind a build tag (go:build meili) as a reference after the migration to Milvus. MeiliClient wraps three meilisearch.IndexManager instances (events, prompts, sessions) and implements SearchSessions, SearchPrompts, SearchEvents, FacetEvents, and ResolveSessionPrefix. Semantic search methods (SemanticSearchPrompts, SemanticSearchEvents, HybridSearch) are stubs returning nil, since MeiliSearch lacks vector search capabilities.
hooks-mcp/internal/meili/types.go: This file defines the canonical Searcher interface and all shared data types used by MCP tool handlers to query the hook event backend. The Searcher interface declares methods for searching sessions, prompts, and events, plus faceting, session prefix resolution, semantic search, hybrid search, and full-text search. It defines option structs SessionSearchOpts, PromptSearchOpts, and EventSearchOpts that carry query parameters, filters, date ranges, and pagination. Hit types SessionHit, PromptHit, and EventHit mirror the document schemas from hooks-store.
hooks-mcp/internal/milvus/client.go: This file implements the meili.Searcher interface against a Milvus vector database REST API v2, serving as the primary search backend for hooks-mcp. MilvusClient provides SearchSessions, SearchPrompts, SearchEvents with LIKE-based text filtering and client-side sorting. Semantic search is supported through SemanticSearchPrompts and SemanticSearchEvents using dense vector search via an Embedder, while HybridSearch combines dense and sparse (BM25) search with server-side RRF reranking.
hooks-mcp/internal/milvus/embedder.go: This file provides the Embedder struct, an HTTP client that calls the embed-svc microservice to convert query text into dense float32 vectors for semantic search in Milvus. The Embed method POSTs a JSON embedRequest to the /embed endpoint and returns the resulting float32 vector. It is used by MilvusClient to generate query embeddings for SemanticSearchPrompts, SemanticSearchEvents, and HybridSearch.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/activity.go: This file registers the project-activity MCP tool, which renders a hierarchical tree of Claude Code sessions grouped by day and submodule for a given project. The makeProjectActivity handler fetches sessions via SearchSessions, groups them by date, and classifies each into a submodule using classifySubmodule based on files_read and files_written paths. Output is formatted as an ASCII tree using format.Tree.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/costs.go: This file registers the cost-analysis MCP tool, which provides cost and token usage analytics across Claude Code sessions. The makeCostAnalysis handler fetches sessions via SearchSessions, computes aggregate totals (cost, input/output tokens), and renders a grouped breakdown using groupByDay, groupBySession, or groupByModel. It also lists the top 5 most expensive sessions.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/errors.go: This file registers the error-analysis MCP tool, which analyzes PostToolUseFailure hook events to surface error frequency by tool name and list recent errors. The makeErrorAnalysis handler queries SearchEvents filtered to HookType PostToolUseFailure, aggregates error counts per tool_name, and renders a bar chart via format.BarChart.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/events.go: This file registers the search-events MCP tool, which performs full-text search across all hook event data stored in Milvus. The makeSearchEvents handler calls FullTextSearchEvents for BM25-ranked results, falling back to LIKE-based SearchEvents on failure. Results include timestamp, session ID, hook type, and context-specific detail.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/helpers.go: Provides shared utility functions and types used across MCP tool handlers. The textResult and errResult functions construct mcp.CallToolResult responses. The commonInputs and resolvedInputs structs standardize session ID and date range parameters, while resolveCommon resolves session ID prefixes via meili.Searcher.ResolveSessionPrefix and parses date range strings via dateparse.ParseRange.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/prompts.go: Implements the query-prompts MCP tool, which retrieves user prompts chronologically with optional filtering by project, session, date range, and full-text query. The makeQueryPrompts handler calls meili.Searcher.SearchPrompts using PromptSearchOpts. Results are grouped by session ID and formatted with truncated prompt text and timestamps.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/recall.go: Implements the recall-context MCP tool, which uses hybrid search (keywords plus semantic similarity via RRF) to find past hook events matching a natural language query. The makeRecallContext handler delegates to meili.Searcher.HybridSearch with EventSearchOpts filters for project, date range, and result limit.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/register.go: Serves as the central registration point for all MCP tools exposed by hooks-mcp. The RegisterAll function registers eleven tools: query-sessions, query-prompts, session-summary, project-activity, search-events, error-analysis, cost-analysis, tool-usage, and three vector-powered tools (semantic-search, recall-context, similar-sessions).
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/semantic.go: Implements the semantic-search MCP tool, which finds past events or prompts that are semantically similar to a natural language query using dense vector embeddings. The makeSemanticSearch handler dispatches to either semanticSearchPrompts or semanticSearchEvents based on the SearchType input field.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/sessions.go: Implements the query-sessions MCP tool, which lists Claude Code sessions filtered by project name, date range, and model, with configurable sort order. Results are rendered as a formatted table with columns for session ID, date, duration, prompt count, event count, cost, model, and a truncated first-prompt preview.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/similar.go: Implements the similar-sessions MCP tool, which finds sessions with similar work patterns by comparing prompt embeddings. The makeSimilarSessions handler resolves the source session, builds a composite query from its prompts, and uses SemanticSearchPrompts to find matches. Results are deduplicated by session ID and ranked by match count and recency.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/summary.go: Implements the session-summary MCP tool, which produces a detailed overview of a single Claude Code session. The output includes header metadata (duration, project, model, cost, tokens), a tool usage bar chart, lists of files read and written, chronological prompts, and error details.
hooks-mcp/internal/tools/toolstats.go: Implements the tool-usage MCP tool, which reports tool distribution and file access patterns across Claude Code sessions. The makeToolUsage handler uses FacetEvents to get tool call distribution and renders it as a bar chart. For multi-session queries it aggregates file access counts and displays top files ranked by frequency.
hooks-monitor/cmd/monitor/main.go: Entry point for the hooks-monitor daemon, a localhost HTTP server that receives and stores Claude Code hook events. Sets up single-instance locking via platform.AcquireLock, registers HTTP endpoints for each hook type, and optionally starts a TUI mode. Supports bearer token authentication, UDS and pub/sub subscriber transports for live event streaming, and graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
hooks-monitor/internal/config/config.go: Manages INI-based configuration for the hooks-monitor, defining which of the 15 canonical hook types (AllHookTypes) are enabled and providing sink and cache settings. ReadConfig parses the [hooks] section with fail-open semantics (missing entries default to enabled). WriteConfig performs atomic file writes preserving non-[hooks] sections. ReadSinkConfig and ReadCacheConfig handle the [sink] and [cache] sections.
hooks-monitor/internal/filecache/filecac: Provides a thread-safe, per-session file metadata cache used to track file read operations within Claude Code sessions. The SessionFileCache maps session IDs to per-session FileRecord entries containing path, mtime, size, and a monotonic read counter. RecordRead stores metadata, Lookup returns cached metadata with a reads-ago staleness metric, and EndSession frees all cached data.
hooks-monitor/internal/hookevt/hookevt.g: Defines the HookEvent struct, the core data type representing a single hook event received from Claude Code. Each HookEvent carries a HookType string identifier, a Timestamp, and a Data map holding the arbitrary JSON payload. This minimal type serves as the shared event schema used across the monitor's HTTP handlers, ring buffer, and TUI.
hooks-monitor/internal/monitor/monitor.g: Implements HookMonitor, a thread-safe bounded ring buffer that stores, counts, and dispatches hook events. AddEvent appends events to the buffer (trimming to MaxEvents/900 when full), increments per-HookType stats, and either forwards to a TUI channel or logs to the console. GetStats and GetEvents provide thread-safe snapshot access to accumulated statistics and recent events.
hooks-monitor/internal/platform/lock.go: Provides the ShowRunningInstance function, which reports diagnostic information about an already-running monitor instance by reading the PID from the lock file and fetching live statistics from the running instance's /stats HTTP endpoint.
hooks-monitor/internal/platform/lock_uni: Unix-specific implementation of AcquireLock that enforces single-instance execution using syscall.Flock with LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB for non-blocking exclusive file locking. On conflict, calls ShowRunningInstance and exits.
hooks-monitor/internal/platform/lock_win: Windows-specific implementation of AcquireLock using windows.LockFileEx with LOCKFILE_EXCLUSIVE_LOCK|LOCKFILE_FAIL_IMMEDIATELY. Mirrors the Unix variant's behavior.
hooks-monitor/internal/platform/signals_: Defines ShutdownSignals for Unix systems: os.Interrupt (Ctrl+C) and syscall.SIGTERM, used by the main function's signal handler for graceful shutdown.
Defines ShutdownSignals for Windows: only os.Interrupt (Ctrl+C) since SIGTERM does not exist on Windows.
hooks-monitor/internal/server/server.go: Implements the HTTP layer for the hooks-monitor, providing handler factories and middleware for the hook ingestion API. HandleHook parses incoming POST JSON payloads with depth checking and deep-clones the data before passing a HookEvent to HookMonitor.AddEvent. SecurityHeaders and AuthMiddleware add response hardening and optional bearer token authentication.
hooks-monitor/internal/subscriber/subscr: Subscriber connects to the hooks-store pub socket to receive real-time hook events and feed them into the monitor's HookMonitor. The Run method maintains a persistent connection with automatic reconnection using exponential backoff (capped at 10 seconds), while connectAndRead handles the MsgSubscribe handshake and reads MsgEvent frames in a loop.
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/detail.go: Provides the detail pane rendering for the hooks-monitor TUI, formatting human-readable views of Session, UserRequest, and EventNode tree nodes via formatNodeDetail. Renders structured sections including Input (tool_input fields like command, file_path, pattern), Context, Data, and Result (paired PostToolUse output). Supports word-wrapping with CJK-aware runewidth and scrollable viewport rendering.
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/hooks_menu.go: Renders the hooks configuration overlay for the monitor TUI, displaying a checklist of all hook types with their enabled/disabled state from config.HookConfig. Invoked when the user presses H in the main TUI.
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/model.go: Core Bubble Tea model for the hooks-monitor TUI, implementing the interactive tree-based event viewer. The Model manages sessions, flattened FlatRow display rows, cursor position, and detail pane state. Supports expand/collapse (h/l/space), detail inspection (i), hooks configuration (H), and vim-style navigation (j/k/g/G).
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/processor.go: EventProcessor is the state machine that organizes incoming HookEvent messages into a hierarchical tree of Session, UserRequest, and EventNode objects. Handles session lifecycle, user prompt grouping, and Pre/Post tool-use pairing via a pendingPre FIFO queue keyed by tool_use_id. Memory is bounded by maxSessions (50) and maxPendingPerKey (50).
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/styles.go: Defines lipgloss visual styles for the hooks-monitor terminal UI. The hookTypeStyles map assigns distinct terminal colors to each hook type. The renderRow function produces styled lines for FlatRow entries with indentation, expand/collapse icons, and rune-safe truncation.
hooks-monitor/internal/tui/tree.go: Defines the core data model types for the hooks-monitor event tree: Session, UserRequest, EventNode (with Pre/Post pairing), and FlatRow. The FlattenTree function walks expanded nodes and produces an ordered slice of FlatRow entries for the TUI's scrollable tree view.
hooks-monitor/internal/udsserver/udsserv: UDSServer accepts hook events and file cache queries over a Unix domain socket in the hooks-monitor process. Handles MsgEvent (both HookEvent envelope and legacy flat JSON) and MsgCacheQuery (file cache lookups against SessionFileCache). Runs via the Serve method until context cancellation.
hooks-store/cmd/hooks-store/main.go: Entry point for the hooks-store service, which ingests Claude Code hook events and persists them to Milvus for search and analytics. Wires together MilvusStore (with optional BufferedStore), HTTP and UDS ingest servers, PubServer for real-time subscribers, and optional SessionFileCache. Supports TUI dashboard or headless mode.
hooks-store/internal/hookevt/hookevt.go: Defines the HookEvent struct used within the hooks-store module as the wire-format representation of Claude Code hook events. Contains HookType, Timestamp, and Data fields. Independent from the monitor's hookevt package — the contract is the JSON schema, not shared Go types.
hooks-store/internal/ingest/server.go: HTTP ingest server that receives hook events via POST /ingest, validates them, transforms them into store.Document via HookEventToDocument, and indexes them into the EventStore. Tracks ingested/error counters atomically and supports an onIngest callback for broadcasting to the TUI and PubServer. Exposes /health and /stats endpoints.
hooks-store/internal/ingest/udsserver.go: UDSIngestServer provides a Unix domain socket alternative to the HTTP ingest path, accepting hook events using the shared framing protocol. Processes MsgEvent messages through the same validation and indexing pipeline as the HTTP server, and handles MsgCacheQuery messages for file read cache lookups via SessionFileCache.
hooks-store/internal/pubsub/pubserver.go: PubServer implements publish-subscribe fan-out over Unix domain sockets for real-time event streaming to subscribers like hooks-monitor. Subscribers connect with a MsgSubscribe handshake, then receive MsgEvent broadcasts with write deadlines and automatic dead-connection cleanup.
hooks-store/internal/store/buffered.go: BufferedStore wraps an EventStore with a ring buffer that captures documents when the underlying store (e.g., Milvus) is unavailable. The Index method falls back to buffering on failure, while a background flushLoop retries buffered documents in batches of 50. The ring buffer overwrites oldest entries when full, tracking drops.
hooks-store/internal/store/embedder.go: Embedder is an HTTP client that calls an external embedding service to generate vector representations of text for semantic search in Milvus. Includes a circuit breaker that opens after 3 consecutive failures, skipping calls for 30 seconds. Returns nil on transient failures so callers can fall back to zero vectors.
hooks-store/internal/store/meili.go: MeiliStore implements the EventStore interface using MeiliSearch as a full-text search backend, guarded by a build tag (go:build meili). Provides migration utilities: MigrateDocuments backfills fields, MigrateDataFlat rewrites search fields, and MigratePrompts populates the prompts index.
hooks-store/internal/store/milvus.go: MilvusStore implements the EventStore interface using Milvus as a vector database backend, managing three collections: events (with dense and BM25 sparse embeddings), prompts, and sessions. The Index method generates dense embeddings via the Embedder and dual-writes UserPromptSubmit events to the prompts collection.
hooks-store/internal/store/milvus_client: MilvusClient is a thin HTTP client for the Milvus REST API v2, providing typed Go methods for collection and entity operations including Insert, Upsert, Query, Search, SearchText (BM25), and HybridSearch. Implements exponential backoff retry and Bearer token authentication.
hooks-store/internal/store/store.go: Defines the core storage types and interface for the hooks-store persistence layer. Document is the search-ready representation with fields for filtering (hook_type, session_id), full-text search (data_flat, prompt), and vector search (dense_embedding). The EventStore interface requires Index and Close methods.
hooks-store/internal/store/transform.go: Transforms wire-format HookEvent objects into search-optimized Document structs via HookEventToDocument, extracting session_id, tool_name, prompt, file_path, error messages, and token/cost metrics from nested event data. The extractStringValues function recursively collects leaf strings for the data_flat full-text search field. DocumentToPromptDocument converts to the lean prompts index format.
hooks-store/internal/tui/model.go: Bubble Tea model for the hooks-store dashboard TUI, displaying real-time ingest metrics and a recent activity log. Shows Milvus connection info, ingested/error counts, last event time, and a color-coded activity feed. Driven by waitForEvent and tickEvery commands.
hooks-store/internal/tui/styles.go: Defines lipgloss terminal styles for the hooks-store dashboard TUI, mirroring the hooks-monitor color palette. The hookTypeStyles map assigns distinct colors to 15 hook types. The hookStyle function performs a map lookup with fallback to defaultHookStyle.
shared/config/config.go: Provides INI-based configuration management for the hooks4claude system with three sections: [hooks] for per-hook-type toggles, [sink] for event forwarding (HTTP vs UDS transport), and [cache] for file read caching. ReadConfig uses fail-open defaults and supports UTF-8 BOM stripping. WriteConfig performs atomic file writes.
shared/filecache/filecache.go: SessionFileCache provides per-session file read tracking to enable deduplication of Read tool calls. RecordRead stores file metadata (mtime, size) keyed by path within a session. Lookup returns whether a file was previously read with a reads-ago staleness metric. Thread-safe via sync.RWMutex.
shared/hookevt/hookevt.go: Defines the shared HookEvent struct used across the hooks4claude system: HookType (string), Timestamp (time.Time), and Data (map[string]interface{}). Provides a common type for packages that need to pass hook events without depending on service-specific internal packages.
shared/uds/uds.go: Implements the Unix domain socket wire protocol shared between hooks-monitor, hooks-store, and hook clients. The framing format is a 5-byte header (1-byte type + 4-byte big-endian length) followed by JSON payload, with 1 MiB max. Defines four message types: MsgEvent, MsgCacheQuery, MsgCacheResponse, and MsgSubscribe. Provides Listen, Dial, and SocketPath utilities.
